name: WP-arch-mock-burndown
description: GC6 burn-down of the internal jest.mock() backlog (INV-2): test files using internal jest.mock('./...') / jest.mock('../...') / jest.mock('@eduagent/...') should convert to the real implementation or jest.requireActual() targeted overrides (canonical pattern: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/archive-cleanup.test.ts). SCOPE FENCE (PRG-11 lane, HARD): EXCLUDE any test file whose module-under-test is in the live IF-cutover surface — identity / consent / family-access / billing / metering / auth / session-exchange / sessions / stripe-webhook / revenuecat-webhook — those collide with CUT-B2/B3 and are deferred post-flip. Per-file judgment at execution.
acceptanceCriteria: A bounded batch of internal-mock test files OUTSIDE the cutover-surface fence converted from internal jest.mock to real impl / jest.requireActual targeted overrides, semantics preserved. Outcome: net reduction in the internal-mock backlog count with no test-behavior change; all touched suites green; the GC1 guard stays green (no NEW internal mocks); external-boundary mocks (LLM via routeAndCall, Stripe, Clerk JWKS, push, email, Inngest framework) are NOT touched. Any cutover-surface files encountered are explicitly listed as deferred (file + reason) in the commit message; a genuinely-unconvertible residual carries a // gc1-allow: <reason>. Batch size is the executor's judgment — bound it so the PR stays reviewable.
